children.heading: .—Separate fund
children.label: 1 .—Separate fund
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), proceeds of the disposition of surplus real and related personal property by the Administrator of General Services shall be set aside in a separate fund in the Treasury.

children.heading: Payment of expenses from the fund.—
children.label: 2 Payment of expenses from the fund.—
i
children.text: Fees of appraisers, auctioneers, and realty brokers, in accordance with the scale customarily paid in similar commercial transactions.

children.label: ii
children.text: Costs of environmental and historic preservation services, highest and best use of property studies, utilization of property studies, deed compliance inspection, and the expenses incurred in a relocation.

children.label: iii
children.text: Advertising and surveying.

children.heading: .—Percentage limitation
children.label: i .—Percentage limitation
children.text: In each fiscal year, no more than 12 percent of the proceeds of all dispositions of surplus real and related personal property may be paid to meet direct expenses incurred in connection with the dispositions.

children.heading: .—Determination of maximum amount
children.label: ii .—Determination of maximum amount
children.text: The Director of the Office of Management and Budget each quarter shall determine the maximum amount that may be obligated under this paragraph.

children.heading: .—Direct payment or reimbursement
children.label: C .—Direct payment or reimbursement
children.text: An amount obligated under this paragraph may be used to pay an expense directly or to reimburse a fund or appropriation that initially paid the expense.

children.heading: .—Transfer to miscellaneous receipts
children.label: 3 .—Transfer to miscellaneous receipts
children.text: At least once each year, excess amounts beyond current operating needs shall be transferred from the fund described in paragraph (1) to miscellaneous receipts.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: 4 .—Report
children.text: A report of receipts, disbursements, and transfers to miscellaneous receipts under this subsection shall be made annually, in connection with the budget estimate, to the Director and to Congress.

children.heading: Real Property Under Control of a Military Department.—
children.label: b Real Property Under Control of a Military Department.—

children.heading: .—Military installation
children.label: A .—Military installation
children.text: The term “military installation” has the meaning given that term in section 2687(e)(1) of title 10.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: .—Base closure law
children.label: B .—Base closure law
children.text: The term “base closure law” has the meaning given that term in .section 101(a)(17) of title 10

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: This subsection applies to real property, including any improvement on the property, that is under the control of a military department and that the Secretary of the department determines is excess to the department’s needs.

children.label: i
children.text: damaged or deteriorated military family housing facilities conveyed under ; orsection 2854a of title 10

children.label: ii
children.text: property at a military installation designated for closure or realignment pursuant to a base closure law.

children.heading: .—Transfer between military departments
children.label: 3 .—Transfer between military departments
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall provide that property described in paragraph (2) is available for transfer, without reimbursement, to other military departments within the Department of Defense.

children.heading: .—Alternative disposition by administrator of general services
children.label: 4 .—Alternative disposition by administrator of general services
children.text: If property is not transferred pursuant to paragraph (3), the Secretary of the military department with the property under its control shall request the Administrator to transfer or dispose of the property in accordance with this subtitle or other applicable law.

children.heading: .—Deposit in special account
children.label: A .—Deposit in special account
children.text: For a transfer or disposition of property pursuant to paragraph (4), the Administrator shall deposit any proceeds (less expenses of the transfer or disposition as provided in subsection (a)) in a special account in the Treasury.

children.label: i
children.text: In the case of property located at a military installation that is closed, the amount is available for facility maintenance and repair or environmental restoration by the military department that had jurisdiction over the property before the closure of the military installation.

children.label: I
children.text: 50 percent of the amount is available for facility maintenance and repair or environmental restoration at the military installation where the property was located before it was disposed of or transferred; and

children.label: II
children.text: 50 percent of the amount is available for facility maintenance and repair and for environmental restoration by the military department that had jurisdiction over the property before it was disposed of or transferred.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: 6 .—Report
children.text: As part of the annual request for authorizations of appropriations to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and the House of Representatives, the Secretary of Defense shall include an accounting of each transfer and disposal made in accordance with this subsection during the fiscal year preceding the fiscal year in which the request is made. The accounting shall include a detailed explanation of each transfer and disposal and of the use of the proceeds received from it by the Department of Defense.
